claim: Obsidian Web Clipper is the official cross-browser extension from the Obsidian team, sits at 4.1k GitHub stars, latest release 1.6.2 (April 23, 2026) with 65 published versions.
quote: Highlight and capture the web in your favorite browser. The official Web Clipper extension for Obsidian.

claim: The official Web Clipper saves to local Markdown, supports highlighting, includes pre-built templates for articles, recipes, references and academic papers, and runs on Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge, Brave, Arc, Orion and Vivaldi.
quote: Highlight and capture web pages in your favorite browser. Save anything and everything with just one click.

claim: Web Clipper's Interpreter feature sends page context plus template prompts to a chosen LLM (BYO key — Claude, GPT, Gemini, or local) and returns values into template variables using the {{"prompt"}} syntax.
quote: When you click interpret, Interpreter sends the page context to your selected model, along with all the prompts in your template in one request.

claim: After Omnivore's shutdown, Obsidian users coalesced around the Web Clipper for archival clipping plus Raindrop.io as a triage inbox, with Raindrop's caveat that it does not store full content as Markdown.
quote: No full content saved in Markdown, as Omnivore was doing.

claim: Omnivore shut down in November 2024 after the team was acquihired by ElevenLabs; the obsidian-omnivore plugin's API is offline and previously synced notes remain but no new sync is possible.
quote: Omnivore shut down in November 2024 after its team was acquihired by ElevenLabs. The service is permanently offline and all user data has been deleted.

claim: The official Readwise Obsidian plugin auto-exports highlights from Kindle, Apple Books, Instapaper, Pocket, Medium, Twitter, PDFs and Readwise Reader into the vault with Jinja2 templating and continuous sync.
Readwise V2 of the Obsidian plugin added mobile sync (iOS/Android) and a Readwise MCP server in 2025 so AI tools (Raycast, Claude) can query highlights directly.
quote: Readwise released an MCP (Model Context Protocol), which can be used to connect other AI tools like Raycast AI with their new MCP feature.

claim: MarkDownload is a non-Obsidian-specific Firefox/Chrome/Edge/Safari extension that converts pages to Markdown files; it can write into a vault folder via the Advanced Obsidian URI plugin to bypass URL-length limits.
quote: A Firefox and Google Chrome extension to clip websites and download them into a readable markdown file.

claim: The Matter Obsidian plugin (128 GitHub stars, last release v1.1.4 November 2022) syncs Matter highlights and notes via QR-code pairing, but the plugin appears inactive with 19 open issues.
quote: The plugin synchronizes all of your Matter highlights and notes directly to your Obsidian vault.

claim: Matter Premium ($60/yr in 2026) is the closest still-maintained equivalent to Omnivore's free tier, bundling newsletter sync, RSS, AI Co-Reader and unlimited Obsidian/Notion highlight export.
quote: Matter Premium at $60/year adds HD text-to-speech, AI Co-Reader, newsletter sync, RSS feeds, unlimited highlights with Notion/Obsidian export.

claim: The Obsidian Hypothesis community plugin syncs Hypothes.is web annotations into the vault on startup or manually, with Nunjucks template formatting.
quote: An Obsidian.md plugin that syncs highlights from Hypothesis.

claim: The lindylearn obsidian-annotations fork adds bi-directional Hypothes.is sync so vault edits propagate back to the web annotations.
quote: Obsidian plugin to sync highlights from Hypothesis bidirectionally.

claim: Email2Obsidian provides a unique forwarding address that buffers messages 72 hours then a vault plugin pulls subjects, bodies, and attachments; free tier covers 10 lifetime emails, Starter is $2.49/mo for 300 monthly emails.
quote: Free Tier: No credit card needed - 10 lifetime emails, Full plugin access with community support

claim: TaskRobin Email Sync archives forwarded emails (and attachments) into Obsidian via a secure forwarding alias, aimed at users who want to keep email artifacts inside the vault.
quote: TaskRobin Email Sync for Obsidian allows users to archive emails and attachments directly into their vault.

claim: The popular RSS Reader plugin by joethei (468 stars) was archived August 25, 2025 with last release v1.2.2 from June 2022, leaving Simple RSS, Local RSS and RSS Dashboard as the actively maintained replacements.
quote: Archived - The repository was archived by the owner on August 25, 2025, and is now read-only.

claim: RSS Dashboard is an actively maintained alternative that aggregates RSS, YouTube channels and podcasts with smart tagging and embedded media playback inside Obsidian.
quote: A dashboard for organizing and consuming RSS feeds, YouTube channels, and podcasts with smart tagging, media playback, and seamless content flow.

claim: Self-hosted Wallabag is the recommended privacy-conscious Omnivore replacement for users who want full data ownership rather than another SaaS.
quote: Wallabag is a stable, proven self-hosted read-later app used by thousands of privacy-conscious readers.
